blocks.body: Present Perfect Continuous: Negatives — haven't / hasn't been + V-ing for actions that are not continuing, not happening lately, or not in progress as expected.

{"level":"A2–B1","intro":"In negative sentences with Present Perfect Continuous, we use have not / has not been + V-ing or the short forms haven't / hasn't been.","rules":[{"label":"haven't / hasn't been + V-ing","color":"emerald","text":"The negative is built with have / has, not with do / does.","example":"I haven't been sleeping well. / She hasn't been studying."},{"label":"Lately / recently","color":"blue","text":"These negatives often say that something has not been happening lately.","example":"We haven't been going out much lately."},{"label":"Unfinished time","color":"amber","text":"The negative also works with today, this week, or for / since when the process has not been happening up to now.","example":"He hasn't been coming to class this week."}]}

{"title":"1. Basic negative models","intro":"Start with have / has, add not, then been and V-ing.","items":[{"label":"I / You / We / They","title":"haven't been + V-ing","subtitle":"I haven't been sleeping well. / They haven't been working."},{"label":"He / She / It","title":"hasn't been + V-ing","subtitle":"She hasn't been waiting long. / It hasn't been working properly."},{"label":"Recent time","title":"lately / recently","subtitle":"I haven't been feeling well lately. / We haven't been seeing them recently."},{"label":"Duration and unfinished periods","title":"for / since / this week","subtitle":"She hasn't been living here for long. / He hasn't been coming this week."}]}

{"title":"2. When you need the negative","sections":[{"label":"The process is not happening","color":"emerald","description":"Show that an action is not continuing or not happening in this period.","examples":[{"en":"I haven't been working much this week.","ua":"The process is limited in this unfinished period."},{"en":"She hasn't been sleeping enough lately.","ua":"This has not been happening recently."}]},{"label":"Problem or change","color":"blue","description":"The negative often describes a situation that has changed or is not going as expected.","examples":[{"en":"We haven't been getting your messages.","ua":"There is a current problem."},{"en":"He hasn't been feeling well recently.","ua":"The situation has been negative lately."}]},{"label":"Unfinished period","color":"amber","description":"Say that in the current period the process has not been happening.","examples":[{"en":"They haven't been coming to the office this month.","ua":"This month is not finished yet."},{"en":"I haven't been using the car lately.","ua":"A recent repeated action is absent."}]}]}

{"title":"3. Quick models","intro":"See how negatives work in this tense.","rows":[{"en":"I haven't been sleeping well lately.","ua":"haven't been sleeping","note":"a state or process lately"},{"en":"She hasn't been working this week.","ua":"hasn't been working","note":"an unfinished period"},{"en":"We haven't been waiting for long.","ua":"haven't been waiting","note":"duration in a negative form"},{"en":"He hasn't been talking much recently.","ua":"hasn't been talking","note":"recent activity is absent"}],"warning":"Do not say I don't been working or She hasn't working."}

{"title":"4. Common mistakes","items":[{"label":"Mistake 1","color":"rose","title":"Using do / does instead of have / has.","wrong":"❌ I don't been sleeping well.","right":"✅ I haven't been sleeping well."},{"label":"Mistake 2","color":"amber","title":"Leaving out been in the formula.","wrong":"❌ She hasn't working lately.","right":"✅ She hasn't been working lately."},{"label":"Mistake 3","color":"sky","title":"Using V1 instead of V-ing.","wrong":"❌ We haven't been wait long.","right":"✅ We haven't been waiting long."}]}

{"title":"5. Short summary","items":["The negative in Present Perfect Continuous = haven't / hasn't been + V-ing.","Use haven't been with I / you / we / they.","Use hasn't been with he / she / it.","These forms often appear with lately, recently, for, since, and unfinished periods like this week.","After been, use the verb with -ing."]}

{"title":"Present Perfect Continuous lessons","items":[{"label":"Forms and Use","url":"/en/theory/present-perfect-continuous/present-perfect-continuous-forms","current":false},{"label":"Negatives","url":"/en/theory/present-perfect-continuous/present-perfect-continuous-negatives","current":true},{"label":"Questions and Short Answers","url":"/en/theory/present-perfect-continuous/present-perfect-continuous-questions","current":false},{"label":"Time Expressions","url":"/en/theory/present-perfect-continuous/present-perfect-continuous-time-expressions","current":false}]}
